name: Suffix automaton — distinct substring count
task: Create /tmp/eval_sam/sam.py with class SAM(s) that builds a suffix automaton of the ASCII string s using online construction (extend one character at a time, maintaining the suffix link / clone-state invariants). Method count_distinct_substrings() returns the number of distinct non-empty substrings of s. The full construction plus the count must be O(|s|) overall (linear in the length of s) — do NOT enumerate or hash substrings. The suffix automaton must support construction up to |s| = 50000 in well under 3 seconds.

validation.script:
import importlib.util, time, random; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_sam/sam.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); SAM = mod.SAM; assert SAM('a').count_distinct_substrings() == 1; assert SAM('aa').count_distinct_substrings() == 2; assert SAM('aaa').count_distinct_substrings() == 3; assert SAM('abc').count_distinct_substrings() == 6; assert SAM('abab').count_distinct_substrings() == 7; assert SAM('abcbc').count_distinct_substrings() == 12; assert SAM('banana').count_distinct_substrings() == 15; assert SAM('mississippi').count_distinct_substrings() == 53
# brute force on random small strings
random.seed(11)
for trial in range(20):
    s = ''.join(random.choice('abc') for _ in range(random.randint(5, 30)))
    brute = len({s[i:j] for i in range(len(s)) for j in range(i+1, len(s)+1)})
    got = SAM(s).count_distinct_substrings()
    assert got == brute, f'string {s!r}: got {got} want {brute}'
# perf test — ensure linear-time
big = ''.join(random.choice('abcde') for _ in range(50000))
start = time.time(); SAM(big).count_distinct_substrings(); el = time.time() - start
assert el < 3.0, f'50000-char SAM took {el:.2f}s — likely not O(n)'
print('OK')
